sentences.text: The atomic memory operation (AMO) instructions perform read-modify-write operations for multiprocessor synchronization and are encoded with an R-type instruction format.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zaamo" Extension for Atomic Memory Operations

sentences.text: These AMO instructions atomically load a data value from the address in rs1, place the value into register rd, apply a binary operator to the loaded value and the original value in rs2, then store the result back to the original address in rs1. AMOs can either operate on doublewords (RV64 only) or words in memory.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zaamo" Extension for Atomic Memory Operations

sentences.text: For RV64, 32-bit AMOs always sign-extend the value placed in rd, and ignore the upper 32 bits of the original value of rs2.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zaamo" Extension for Atomic Memory Operations

sentences.text: For AMOs, the Zaamo extension requires that the address held in rs1 be naturally aligned to the size of the operand (i.e., eight-byte aligned for doublewords and four-byte aligned for words).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zaamo" Extension for Atomic Memory Operations

sentences.text: If the address is not naturally aligned, an address-misaligned exception or an access-fault exception will be generated.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zaamo" Extension for Atomic Memory Operations

sentences.text: The access-fault exception can be generated for a memory access that would otherwise be able to complete except for the misalignment, if the misaligned access should not be emulated.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zaamo" Extension for Atomic Memory Operations

sentences.text: The misaligned atomicity granule PMA, defined in , optionally relaxes this alignment requirement.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zaamo" Extension for Atomic Memory Operations

sentences.text: If present, the misaligned atomicity granule PMA specifies the size of a misaligned atomicity granule, a power-of-two number of bytes.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zaamo" Extension for Atomic Memory Operations

sentences.text: The misaligned atomicity granule PMA applies only to AMOs, loads and stores defined in the base ISAs, and loads and stores of no more than XLEN bits defined in the F, D, and Q extensions, and compressed encodings thereof.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zaamo" Extension for Atomic Memory Operations

sentences.text: For an instruction in that set, if all accessed bytes lie within the same misaligned atomicity granule, the instruction will not raise an exception for reasons of address alignment, and the instruction will give rise to only one memory operation for the purposes of RVWMO--i.e., it will execute atomically.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zaamo" Extension for Atomic Memory Operations

sentences.text: The operations supported are swap, integer add, bitwise AND, bitwise OR, bitwise XOR, and signed and unsigned integer maximum and minimum.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zaamo" Extension for Atomic Memory Operations

sentences.text: Without ordering constraints, these AMOs can be used to implement parallel reduction operations, where typically the return value would be discarded by writing to `x0`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zaamo" Extension for Atomic Memory Operations

sentences.text: To help implement multiprocessor synchronization, the AMOs optionally provide release consistency semantics.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zaamo" Extension for Atomic Memory Operations

sentences.text: If the aq bit is set, then no later memory operations in this RISC-V hart can be observed to take place before the AMO.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zaamo" Extension for Atomic Memory Operations

sentences.text: Conversely, if the rl bit is set, then other RISC-V harts will not observe the AMO before memory accesses preceding the AMO in this RISC-V hart.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zaamo" Extension for Atomic Memory Operations

sentences.text: Setting both the aq and the rl bit on an AMO makes the sequence sequentially consistent, meaning that it cannot be reordered with earlier or later memory operations from the same hart.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zaamo" Extension for Atomic Memory Operations
